steps.formal: builder.validateResponse StepResults([object] %stepResults%), Goal([object] %goal%) | error.handle Actions([list<action>] [goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"LlmFixer"})])

steps.guidance: Enrich response (backfill keep:true steps, tag sources) and write back to %stepResults%. Keep @known mapping.
steps.formal: builder.enrichResponse StepResults([object] %stepResults%), Goal([object] %goal%) | variable.set Name([string] %stepResults%), Value([object] %__data__%)

steps.guidance: Initialize %currentPass% as empty JSON object. Keep @known mapping.
Set %currentPass.goal% = %goal% (object). Keep @known mapping.
Set %currentPass.system% = %buildGoalPrompt% (object). Keep @known mapping.
Set %currentPass.user% = %goalForLlm% (object). Keep @known mapping.
Set %currentPass.response% = %stepResults% (object). Keep @known mapping.
Compute elapsed ticks: %passElapsedTicks% = %Now.Ticks% - %passStart%. Keep @known mapping.
steps.formal: math.subtract A([object] %Now.Ticks%), B([object] %passStart%) | variable.set Name([string] %passElapsedTicks%), Value([object] %__data__%)

steps.guidance: Convert ticks to ms: %passDurationMs% = %passElapsedTicks% / 10000. Keep @known mapping.
steps.formal: math.divide A([object] %passElapsedTicks%), B([object] 10000) | variable.set Name([string] %passDurationMs%), Value([object] %__data__%)

steps.guidance: Set %currentPass.durationMs% = %passDurationMs%. Keep @known mapping.
Group %stepResults.steps% by property 'level' into %groupedSteps%. Keep @known mapping.
steps.formal: list.group ListName([string] %stepResults.steps%), Key([string] level) | variable.set Name([string] %groupedSteps%), Value([object] %__data__%)

steps.guidance: Iterate grouped steps and call ProcessGroup for each group. Fixes: goal.call payload must be {"name":"ProcessGroup"} and parameters should be passed via goal.call parameters list (not the invalid expanded object from @known). Also Actor should be omitted or set to Actor([actor] this?)—since the catalog only allows Actor([actor?]) and the provided @known is invalid, omit Actor.
steps.formal: loop.foreach Collection([object] %groupedSteps%), ItemName([string] group) | goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"ProcessGroup"})

steps.comment:
Builds a single sub-goal — no version, no sub-goal recursion, no save.
Adds its pass data to the parent's %trace.subGoals%.

steps.guidance: Append %currentPass% into the parent's %trace.subGoals% list using list.add. This mutates the trace collection for later reporting.

value.system:
# Goal Builder

You are the PLang compiler. Map each step in a goal to engine actions.

## How the Engine Works

The engine has **modules** (namespaces) with **action classes**. Each action class has properties and a `Run()` method.

Your job: for each step, produce `{module, action, parameters}` where:
- `module` = namespace (output, file, goal, condition, variable, etc.)
- `action` = class name (write, read, call, if, set, etc.)
- `parameters` = list of `{name, value, type}` matching the class properties

The engine does: find class → set properties → call Run().

## Type Information

Primitive types: string, int, long, float, double, decimal, bool, datetime, time, guid, byte, bytes, list, array, dictionary, object, json

Special types: `tstring` = translatable string; `any` = untyped/dynamic value.

Types referenced by the catalog below:
  goal.call: { name: string, parallel: bool, parameters: list<object> }
  actor: user | service | system
  operator: == | != | > | < | >= | <= | contains | startswith | endswith | in | isempty | and | or
  step: { index: int, text: string, indent: int, comment: string, intent: string }
  action: { module: string, actionName: string, parameters: list<object> }
  mockhandle: { id: string, actionPattern: string, callCount: int, isSpy: bool }
  llmmessage: { role: string, content: string, images: list<string> }
  httpmethod: GET | POST | PUT | DELETE | PATCH | HEAD | OPTIONS | QUERY
  streamformat: Line | SSE | Bytes
  contentas: File | Base64 | Form | Text
  path: constructor(rawPath: string), properties: extension(string), fileName(string), fileNameWithoutExtension(string), directory(string), mimeType(string), isFile(bool), isDirectory(bool), exists(bool), size(long) (e.g. /some/file.json)
  goal: { name: string, description: string, comment: string, visibility: visibility, inputParameters: dict<string,string> }
  errororder: GoalFirst | RetryFirst
  eventtype: BeforeAppStart | AfterAppStart | BeforeGoal | AfterGoal | BeforeStep | AfterStep | OnError | OnVariableChange | OnBeforeGoalLoad | OnAfterGoalLoad | OnBeforeStepLoad | OnAfterStepLoad | BeforeAction | AfterAction | OnCacheHit | OnCacheMiss
  results: { count: int }
  testfile: { path: string, prPath: string, entryGoalName: string, status: teststatus }
  visibility: Private | Public
  teststatus: Ready | Pass | Fail | Timeout | Stale | Skipped

## Available Actions

Actions below map PLang step text to engine calls. Each entry: `/ description` then `- module.action Params`.
Notation: `?` = optional, `=val` = default, `%var%` = runtime variable reference.


## app — Run a goal, step, or action on a specified actor

/ Run a goal, step, or individual action, optionally switching to another actor
- app.run GoalName([goal.call?]), Step([step?]), Action([action?]), Actor([actor?])



## assert — Test assertions that fail the step with a descriptive error when the condition is not met

/ Assert that Value does not contain Container; fails with an error if it does
- assert.notContains Value([object?]), Container([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that A is less than B; fails with an error if not
- assert.lessThan A([object?]), B([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value is falsy (false, 0, null, or empty); fails with an error if truthy
- assert.isFalse Value([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that A is greater than B; fails with an error if not
- assert.greaterThan A([object?]), B([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Expected does not equal Actual; fails with an error if they match
- assert.notEquals Expected([object?]), Actual([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value is truthy (non-null, non-zero, non-empty); fails with an error if falsy
- assert.isTrue Value([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value is null or unset; fails with an error if it has a value
- assert.isNull Value([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value contains Container; fails with an error if not
- assert.contains Value([object?]), Container([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value is not null or empty; fails with an error if null
- assert.isNotNull Value([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Expected equals Actual; fails with an error if they differ
- assert.equals Expected([object?]), Actual([object?]), Message([string?])



## builder — Builder internals: load, merge, validate, and save goal and step data during the build pipeline

/ Persist the built app artifact to disk after a successful build run
- builder.appSave

/ Retrieve the registered action catalog for use in the builder prompt
- builder.actions

/ Load the app-level build context from a directory path
- builder.app Path([string = "."])

/ Validate an action set against known modules and parameter schemas
- builder.validate Actions([list<action>?])

/ Return the list of registered PLang type names available to the builder
- builder.types

/ Save a built goal's .pr file to disk, completing the build for that goal
- builder.goalsSave Goal([goal])

/ Enriches LLM build response: backfills actions for keep:true steps from prior .pr, tags each step with source (new|known|hint)
- builder.enrichResponse StepResults([buildresponse]), Goal([goal])

/ Load all goal files from a directory for processing by the builder
- builder.goals Path([string])

/ Promote grouped sub-steps into top-level steps for correct inline step handling
- builder.promoteGroups Steps([object])

/ Validate an LLM build response for structural integrity — step count, indexes, empty actions, keep:true with no prior
- builder.validateResponse StepResults([buildresponse]), Goal([goal])

/ Merge an LLM-generated step result onto the existing step, preserving runtime fields
- builder.merge Step([step]), StepFromLlm([step])



## condition — Conditional branching: evaluate comparisons and branch execution with if/elseif/else

/ Compare two values with an operator and write the boolean result to a variable
- condition.compare Left([object?]), Operator([operator]), Right([object?])
  e.g. `compare %a% > %b%, write to %isGreater` → condition.compare Left([object] %a%), Operator([operator] >), Right([object] %b%) | variable.set Name([string] %isGreater%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Evaluate a condition and execute the then-branch actions; pair with elseif/else for full branching
- condition.if Left([object?]), Operator([operator]), Right([object?]), Negate([bool = false])
  e.g. `if %count% > 0, call ProcessItems` → condition.if Left([object] %count%), Operator([operator] >), Right([int] 0) | goal.call GoalName([goal.call] ProcessItems)

/ Additional condition branch evaluated when the preceding if condition is false
- condition.elseif Left([object?]), Operator([operator]), Right([object?]), Negate([bool = false])
  e.g. `else if %a% > 5, write 'mid'` → condition.elseif Left([object] %a%), Operator([operator] >), Right([int] 5)

/ Fallback branch that executes when all preceding if/elseif conditions are false
- condition.else



## crypto — Cryptographic hashing and hash verification using configurable algorithms

/ Verify that Data matches a previously computed Hash using the specified algorithm
- crypto.verify Data([object]), Hash([string]), Algorithm([string = "keccak256"])
  e.g. `verify %content% against %hash%, write to %isValid` → crypto.verify Data([object] %content%), Hash([string] %hash%), Algorithm([string] keccak256) | variable.set Name([string] %isValid%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Hash data using the specified algorithm (default keccak256) and return the hex digest
- crypto.hash Data([object]), Algorithm([string = "keccak256"])
  e.g. `hash %content%, write to %hash` → crypto.hash Data([object] %content%), Algorithm([string] keccak256) | variable.set Name([string] %hash%), Value([object] %__data__%)



## error — Error handling: throw errors from a step, or wrap the preceding action with retry/handle-actions/ignore semantics

/ Immediately fail the step with a structured error message, status code, and optional key
- error.throw Message([string]), StatusCode([int = 500]), Key([string?])



## event — Lifecycle event hooks: register callbacks that run before or after goals, steps, or actions

/ Unregister a previously registered lifecycle event binding by its ID
- event.remove EventId([string])

/ Skip the intercepted action and return a custom value from inside a beforeAction event handler
- event.skipAction Value([object])
  e.g. `skip action, value = %mockResponse` → event.skipAction Value([object] %mockResponse%)

/ Register a goal callback to fire at a lifecycle event (BeforeGoal, AfterStep, BeforeAction, etc.)
- event.on Type([eventtype]), GoalToCall([goal.call]), GoalPattern([string?]), StepPattern([string?]), ActionPattern([string?]), IsRegex([bool = false]), Priority([int = 0]), Actor([actor?])
  e.g. `before step, call LogStep, on goal pattern 'Api/*'` → event.on Type([eventtype] BeforeStep), GoalToCall([goal.call] LogStep), GoalPattern([string] Api/*)



## file — Read, write, copy, move, delete, and list files through the configured filesystem abstraction

/ Delete a file or directory at Path, optionally recursively or ignoring missing targets
- file.delete Path([path]), IgnoreIfNotFound([bool = false]), Recursive([bool = false])

/ Copy a file or folder from Source to Destination, optionally overwriting and including subfolders
- file.copy Source([path]), Destination([path]), Overwrite([bool = false]), IncludeSubfolders([bool = true])

/ Read a file's content; optionally resolve %var% patterns in the text before returning
- file.read Path([path]), ResolveVariables([bool = false])
  e.g. `read file.txt, write to %content` → file.read Path([path] file.txt) | variable.set Name([string] %content%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Check whether a file or directory exists at Path and return file info
- file.exists Path([path])
  e.g. `check if file.txt exists, write to %fileInfo` → file.exists Path([path] file.txt) | variable.set Name([string] %fileInfo%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Write Value to a file at Path, creating directories as needed
- file.save Path([path]), Value([object?])

/ List files in a directory matching an optional glob pattern, optionally recursing into subdirectories
- file.list Path([path]), Pattern([string = "*"]), Recursive([bool = false])
  e.g. `list files in docs/ recursive, write to %files` → file.list Path([path] docs/), Recursive([bool] true) | variable.set Name([string] %files%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Move or rename a file from Source to Destination, optionally overwriting the target
- file.move Source([path]), Destination([path]), Overwrite([bool = false])



## goal — Call and return from goals, enabling goal composition and early exit with a value

/ Return early from the current goal, propagating Data as the goal result
- goal.return Data([object?]), Depth([int = 1])

/ Call a named goal, injecting parameters into its context, optionally on a different actor
- goal.call GoalName([goal.call]), Actor([actor?])



## http — HTTP client: send requests, upload/download files, and configure defaults like base URL and headers

/ Send an HTTP request (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE etc.) and return the parsed response body
- http.request Url([string]), Method([httpmethod = GET]), Body([object?]), Headers([dict<string,object>?]), ContentType([string = "application/json"]), Encoding([string = "utf-8"]), TimeoutInSec([int = 30]), Unsigned([bool = false]), SignOptions([sign?]), OnStream([goal.call?]), StreamAs([streamformat?])

/ Download bytes from a URL and return them in Data; chain with file.save to write to disk
- http.download Url([string]), Headers([dict<string,object>?]), TimeoutInSec([int = 30]), Unsigned([bool = false]), SignOptions([sign?]), OnProgress([goal.call?])

/ Upload content (file, form data, JSON, or bytes) to a URL via multipart or body POST
- http.upload Url([string]), Content([object]), Method([httpmethod = POST]), Headers([dict<string,object>?]), Encoding([string = "utf-8"]), TimeoutInSec([int = 30]), Unsigned([bool = false]), SignOptions([sign?]), As([contentas?]), OnProgress([goal.call?])

/ Set HTTP module defaults (base URL, timeout, headers, content type) for the current scope
- http.configure TimeoutInSec([int?]), BaseUrl([string?]), DefaultHeaders([dict<string,object>?]), ContentType([string?]), Encoding([string?]), Unsigned([bool?]), FollowRedirects([bool?]), MaxRedirects([int?]), Default([bool = false])



## identity — Manage cryptographic identities: create, list, set default, rename, archive, and export keys

/ Retrieve an identity by name, or the current default identity if Name is omitted
- identity.get Name([string?])

/ Restore a previously archived identity, making it active and visible again
- identity.unarchive Name([string])

/ Switch the default identity to the named one; only one identity can be default at a time
- identity.setDefault Name([string])

/ Export the private key of an identity as a raw string
- identity.export Name([string?])

/ Create a new named identity with a generated key pair, optionally setting it as default
- identity.create Name([string = "default"]), SetAsDefault([bool = false]), Provider([string?])

/ List all active (non-archived) identities in the store
- identity.list

/ Rename an identity from Name to NewName, preserving its key pair
- identity.rename Name([string]), NewName([string])

/ Soft-delete an identity by archiving it; hidden from listings but keys are preserved
- identity.archive Name([string]), Force([bool = false])



## list — Inspect and manipulate in-memory lists: add, remove, sort, search, split, join, and group items

/ Append or insert a value into a list variable at an optional index
- list.add ListName([%var% string]), Value([object]), AtIndex([int = -1])

/ Split a string into a list using a separator (default comma), optionally removing empty entries
- list.split Value([string]), Separator([string = ","]), RemoveEmpty([bool = false])

/ Return true if any item in the list has a property matching the given operator and value
- list.any ListName([%var% string]), Key([string]), Operator([operator]), Value([object])

/ Replace the item at a zero-based Index in the list with Value
- list.set ListName([%var% string]), Index([int]), Value([object])

/ Generate a list of integers from Start to End inclusive, stepping by Step (default 1)
- list.range Start([int]), End([int]), Step([int = 1])

/ Return the last item of the list, or empty Data if the list is empty
- list.last ListName([%var% string])

/ Concatenate all list items into a single string, separated by Separator (default comma)
- list.join ListName([%var% string]), Separator([string = ","])

/ Reverse the order of items in the list in place
- list.reverse ListName([%var% string])

/ Sort the list in place ascending by default, or descending when Descending is true
- list.sort ListName([%var% string]), Descending([bool = false])

/ Remove duplicate items from the list and return the deduplicated result
- list.unique ListName([%var% string])

/ Remove an item from the list by value or by zero-based index
- list.remove ListName([%var% string]), Value([object]), AtIndex([int = -1])

/ Return the number of items in the list
- list.count ListName([%var% string])

/ Recursively flatten nested lists into a single flat list
- list.flatten ListName([%var% string])

/ Return the zero-based index of the first item equal to Value, or -1 if not found
- list.indexof ListName([%var% string]), Value([object])

/ Return true if the list contains an item equal to Value
- list.contains ListName([%var% string]), Value([object])

/ Return the first item of the list, or empty Data if the list is empty
- list.first ListName([%var% string])

/ Group list items by a property key, returning [{key, steps}] buckets
- list.group ListName([%var% string]), Key([string])

/ Return the item at a zero-based Index from the list
- list.get ListName([%var% string]), Index([int])



## llm — Send prompts to an LLM and receive structured or streamed responses, with optional tool use

/ Send a conversation (system + user messages) to an LLM and return the response, with optional schema or tool use
- llm.query Messages([list<llmmessage>]), Tools([list<goal.call>?]), OnToolCall([goal.call?]), OnValidateResponse([goal.call?]), OnStream([goal.call?]), Schema([string?]), Format([string?]), Model([string?]), ContinuePreviousConversation([bool = false]), Temperature([double = 0]), TopP([double?]), MaxTokens([int = 16000]), MaxToolCalls([int = 10]), MaxValidationRetries([int = 0]), Cache([bool = true])
  e.g. `system: analyze sentiment, user: %comment%, schema: {sentiment: string}, write to %result` → llm.query Messages([list<LlmMessage>] [{"Role":"system","Content":"analyze sentiment"},{"Role":"user","Content":"%comment%"}]), Schema([string] {sentiment: string}) | variable.set Name([string] %result%), Value([object] %__data__%)



## loop — Iterate over a collection, executing the remaining step actions once per item

/ Iterate over Collection, binding each item to ItemName and executing the remaining step actions
- loop.foreach Collection([object]), ItemName([%var% string?]), KeyName([%var% string?])
  e.g. `foreach %items%, call ProcessItem item=%item` → loop.foreach Collection([object] %items%), ItemName([string] item) | goal.call GoalName([goal.call] ProcessItem)



## math — Arithmetic operations on numeric values: add, subtract, multiply, divide, round, and more

/ Return the absolute (non-negative) value of a number
- math.abs Value([object])

/ Multiply A by B and return the numeric result
- math.multiply A([object]), B([object])

/ Return the largest integer less than or equal to Value (floor)
- math.floor Value([object])

/ Return the smaller of A and B
- math.min A([object]), B([object])

/ Round Value to the specified number of Decimals (default 0, ties away from zero)
- math.round Value([object]), Decimals([int = 0])

/ Return the smallest integer greater than or equal to Value (ceiling)
- math.ceiling Value([object])

/ Raise Base to the power of Exponent
- math.power Base([object]), Exponent([object])

/ Return the larger of A and B
- math.max A([object]), B([object])

/ Add A and B together and return the numeric result
- math.add A([object]), B([object])

/ Return a random integer between Min and Max inclusive (defaults 0–100)
- math.random Min([int = 0]), Max([int = 100])

/ Divide A by B and return the result; fails on division by zero
- math.divide A([object]), B([object])

/ Return the square root of Value; fails for negative inputs
- math.sqrt Value([object])

/ Return the remainder of A divided by B
- math.modulo A([object]), B([object])

/ Subtract B from A and return the numeric result
- math.subtract A([object]), B([object])



## mock — Test mocking: intercept actions by pattern and replace them with fixed responses or spy on calls

/ Assert that a mock was called exactly ExpectedCount times; fails the step if the count differs
- mock.verify Mock([mockhandle]), ExpectedCount([int]), Message([string?])

/ Remove a specific mock or all active mocks, clearing their interceptors and call history
- mock.reset Mock([mockhandle?])

/ Intercept actions matching a pattern and replace their result with a fixed value or goal call
- mock.intercept ActionPattern([string]), ReturnValue([object?]), GoalToCall([goal.call?]), Parameters([dict<string,object>?])



## module — Load and unload external module DLLs at runtime to extend the available action catalog

/ Unregister all actions of a named module from the current app's action catalog
- module.remove Name([string])

/ Load a module DLL from Path and register its actions into the current app's action catalog
- module.add Path([string]), Namespace([string?])



## output — Send text or data to an output channel (console, trace, UI, etc.)

/ Write Data to the actor's current output channel (default: console or configured channel)
- output.write Data([object])



## provider — Manage the provider registry: list, load, remove, and change defaults for signing, crypto, etc.

/ Set a named provider as the default for its type (signing, crypto, identity, key)
- provider.setDefault Name([string]), Type([string?])

/ Remove a named provider from the registry, optionally filtering by provider type
- provider.remove Name([string]), Type([string?])

/ Load a provider from a DLL path and register it in the app's provider registry
- provider.load Path([string?]), Name([string?])

/ List registered providers, optionally filtered by type (signing, crypto, identity, key)
- provider.list Type([string?])



## settings — Persistent key-value settings store backed by the System actor's data source

/ Retrieve a settings value by Key from the System actor's persistent settings store
- settings.get Key([string])

/ Delete a settings entry by Key from the System actor's persistent settings store
- settings.remove Key([string])

/ Persist a key-value pair in the System actor's settings store
- settings.set Key([string]), Value([object?])



## signing — Sign and verify data payloads using pluggable cryptographic providers

/ Sign a data payload and return a signed envelope via the configured signing provider
- signing.sign Data([object?]), Contracts([list<string>?]), Headers([dict<string,object>?]), ExpiresInMs([int?])

/ Verify a signed envelope against expected contracts and headers
- signing.verify Data([object?]), Contracts([list<string>?]), Headers([dict<string,object>?]), TimeoutMs([long?])



## test — Discover, run, and report on PLang test goals with tag filtering and coverage tracking

/ Write test results to the console and emit a JSON or JUnit report artefact
- test.report Results([results?]), Format([string?])
  e.g. `write test report %results% format='junit', write to %report` → test.report Results([object] %results%), Format([string] junit) | variable.set Name([string] %report%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Execute a list of discovered TestFiles in parallel and accumulate results
- test.run Tests([list<testfile>]), Parallel([int?]), Timeout([int?])
  e.g. `run tests %tests%, write to %results` → test.run Tests([object] %tests%) | variable.set Name([string] %results%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Walk a directory for *.test.goal files and return a filtered list of TestFile descriptors
- test.discover Path([string = "."]), Pattern([string = "*.test.goal"]), Recursive([bool = true])
  e.g. `discover tests in 'Tests/Foo' recursive=false, write to %tests` → test.discover Path([string] Tests/Foo), Recursive([bool] false) | variable.set Name([string] %tests%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Attach user-defined tags to the currently running test for filtering and reporting
- test.tag Tags([list<string>])
  e.g. `tag this test 'http', 'fast'` → test.tag Tags([list<string>] ["http", "fast"])



## timer — Named stopwatch timers for measuring elapsed time across steps

/ Stop a named timer and return the elapsed TimeSpan since it was started
- timer.end Name([string?])

/ Record a named start time so a later timer.end can compute elapsed duration
- timer.start Name([string?]), Scope([string = "goal"])

/ Pause execution for Ms milliseconds, honouring the current cancellation token
- timer.sleep Ms([int])



## ui — Render Liquid templates from inline content or files with PLang variable injection

/ Render a Liquid template (file or inline) with the current variables and optional explicit parameters
- ui.render Template([string]), Parameters([list<object>?]), IsFile([bool?])
  e.g. `render 'page.html' with title=%pageTitle%, write to %html` → ui.render Template([string] page.html), Parameters([list<object>] [{"Name":"title","Value":"%pageTitle%"}]) | variable.set Name([string] %html%), Value([object] %__data__%)



## variable — Read, write, and inspect PLang runtime variables in the current scope

/ Retrieve a variable's current value from the active scope
- variable.get Name([%var% string])

/ Delete a named variable from the current scope
- variable.remove Name([%var% string])

/ Remove all variables from the current scope
- variable.clear

/ Assign a value to a named variable, optionally coercing to a type or setting only when unset
- variable.set Name([%var% string]), Value([object]), Type([string?]), AsDefault([bool = false])
  e.g. `set %data% = {"name": "%user%", "age": 30}, type=json` → variable.set Name([string] %data%), Value([json] {"name":"%user%","age":30}), Type([string] json)

/ Return true if a named variable exists and has been initialized in the current scope
- variable.exists Name([%var% string])



# Modifiers

Modifiers wrap the preceding action in the same step. They never stand alone — a step that starts with a modifier is invalid. In `formal`, a modifier appears as an extra pipe segment right after the action it wraps: `file.read Path(...) | cache.wrap DurationMs(...)`.

## cache — Modifier that wraps the preceding action with result caching for a given duration

/ Cache the result of the preceding action for DurationMs milliseconds, skipping re-execution on hit
- cache.wrap DurationMs([long]), Sliding([bool = false]), Key([string?])


## error — Error handling: throw errors from a step, or wrap the preceding action with retry/handle-actions/ignore semantics

/ Intercept errors from the preceding action; optionally retry, run a recovery action chain, or suppress the error
- error.handle StatusCode([int?]), Key([string?]), Message([string?]), Actions([list<action>?]), RetryCount([int?]), RetryOverMs([int?]), Order([errororder?]), IgnoreError([bool = false])
  e.g. `read %path%, on error key 404, write out "missing", read fallback.txt, write to %content` → file.read Path([path] %path%) | error.handle Key([string] 404), Actions([list<action>] [{"module":"output","action":"write","parameters":[{"name":"Data","value":"missing","type":"object"}]},{"module":"file","action":"read","parameters":[{"name":"Path","value":"fallback.txt","type":"path"}]},{"module":"variable","action":"set","parameters":[{"name":"Name","value":"%content%","type":"string"},{"name":"Value","value":"%__data__%","type":"object"}]}])


## timeout — Modifier that wraps any action with a hard deadline, returning a 408 error on expiry

/ Cancel the decorated action and return a timeout error if it exceeds Ms milliseconds
- timeout.after Ms([int])




## Response Format

For each step, produce (in this order):
1. `guidance` — your reasoning: what module.action, why, any concerns
2. `formal` — the action chain in compact shorthand (see Formal Syntax below). Write this BEFORE `actions` — commit to a shape, then serialize it as JSON.
3. `actions` — the mapped actions with parameters. Must agree with `formal`: same modules, same actions, same params in the same order.
4. `level` + `confidence` — how sure you are
5. `group` (optional) — group name for related steps that should be built together

## Formal Syntax

`formal` is the same notation the action catalog uses, but with actual values filled in.

Shape per action: `module.action Name([type] value), Name([type] value), ...`

- Params are comma-separated within an action.
- Actions are pipe-separated (` | `) within a step. One step → one `formal` string, possibly with multiple pipe segments.
- `[type]` tag precedes the value. Same types the catalog uses (`string`, `path`, `int`, `bool`, `object`, `%var% string`, `goal.call`, etc.).
- String values that contain spaces or commas are quoted with double quotes. Numbers, bools, and `%var%` references are unquoted.
- Structured values (e.g. a `goal.call` payload) stay as inline JSON right after the type tag: `GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"Greet","parameters":[...]})`.
- Modifiers (`error.handle`, `cache.wrap`, `timeout.after`) are extra pipe segments right after the action they wrap.

Examples of `formal` for common steps:

```
step   : read file 'config.json', write to %config%
formal : file.read Path([path] config.json) | variable.set Name([string] %config%), Value([object] %__data__%)

step   : call goal Greet name="world"
formal : goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"Greet","parameters":[{"name":"name","value":"world","type":"string"}]})

step   : call Save, on error retry 2 times, then call HandleError
formal : goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"Save"}) | error.handle RetryCount([int] 2), Order([string] "RetryFirst"), Actions([list<action>] [goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"HandleError"})])
```

### `goal.call` payloads — the `name` field is a goal name, not a type

The `goal.call` JSON `{"name": "..."}` always carries the **name of a goal** — a short identifier that matches a `.goal` file or a sibling sub-goal in the same file. Examples: `"BuildGoalCore"`, `"HandleValidationError"`, `"Setup/Init"`. Goal names never contain dots.

**Never put a CLR/library type name in this slot.** When a step text says "call X" or "on error call X", the literal token X *is* the goal name — copy it verbatim. Don't translate it through any framework name. Common wrong shapes (catch yourself if you write any of these): `App.Goals.Goal.GoalCall`, `Fluid.Values.ObjectDictionaryFluidIndexable`, anything `Some.Namespace.SomeType`. If the @known mapping shows `{"Name":"Foo"}`, emit `{"name":"Foo"}` — same name. Do not invent.

```
step   : call BuildGoalCore, on error HandleBuildGoalFailure
formal : goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"BuildGoalCore"}) | error.handle Actions([list<action>] [goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"HandleBuildGoalFailure"})])
```

`error.handle` only takes `Actions` (a `list<action>`) for recovery — never wrap a single goal call in a special parameter slot. A bare `on error call Y` becomes `Actions=[goal.call GoalName({"name":"Y"})]`, the same shape used for any action chain.

Levels:
- **high** (80-100%) — fully built, all parameters
- **medium** (40-79%) — module/action identified, parameters uncertain
- **low** (0-39%) — best guess

## Prior Build Hints — `@known:`

A step may arrive with a trailing `@known: <actions>` annotation emitted by the builder (not by the developer). This is metadata — not step text, not an operator, not a comparison. It means the builder confirmed the step text is unchanged since this mapping was produced, so the prior mapping is authoritative.

### Response contract for `@known`

```
FOR a step annotated with @known:
  EMIT EXACTLY:   { "index": N, "keep": true }
  OMIT:           guidance, formal, actions, level, confidence, group
  DO NOT:         include an "actions" key — not an empty array, not the prior
                  actions, not anything. The engine will reuse the prior mapping
                  from its own .pr on disk.
```

Reproduction is not "safer" or "more explicit" — it wastes tokens, risks drift, and violates the contract. The prior mapping is authoritative.

A step without `@known:` is new or edited — build it from scratch as a normal full response. The builder only emits `@known:` when the step text matches the prior .pr exactly; any text change (even a typo fix) drops the prior mapping and you see an unannotated step.

**Cross-step reinforcement.** Treat `@known` entries elsewhere in the goal as canonical examples of this developer's style. When a new step expresses the same intent as a neighboring known step, map it the same way.

**When in doubt, emit a full response.** Omission (`keep: true`) is the confident-no-op signal, not a fallback. Never emit `keep: true` on a step that lacked `@known:`; never combine `keep: true` with `actions`.

## Step Grouping

When steps are logically related and understanding one helps build the others, assign them the same `group` name. If any step in a group needs the detail pass (medium/low), ALL steps in the group are sent together so the LLM sees the full flow.

Group when:
- Steps form a multi-step pattern (e.g., read file → process → write result)
- A condition step and its indented sub-steps are related
- Steps share variables or data flow between them
- Error handling spans multiple steps

Do NOT group unrelated steps. Steps without a group are processed individually. Use short descriptive names: "file-processing", "auth-flow", "data-transform", etc.

## Example

Goal:
```plang
Start
- call goal Greet name="world"
- read file 'config.json', write to %config%
    cache for 5 minutes
- if %config.debug% is true
    - write out "debug mode"
- foreach %config.items%, call ProcessItem item=%item%
- call Save, on error retry 2 times, then call HandleError
```

## Steps Are Chains of Actions

A natural step usually compiles to more than one action. Each clause in the step text that **does work** — returns data, mutates state, branches, iterates, wraps — is its own action. Chain them in `formal` with ` | `; they execute in order, and the producing action's result is the consuming action's `%__data__%` input.

Walk the step text left to right. For each clause, ask: *does this do work, or is it a description of what the preceding work should do?* If it does work, it's an action. If it modifies how the preceding work runs (error handling, caching, deadlines), it's a modifier action right after it.

Illustrative patterns (not exhaustive — any clause-doing-work is an action):

- **`… write to %var%`** → main action | `variable.set Name=%var%, Value=%__data__%`. Applies to any producing action that assigns its output to a variable.
- **`… write out …`** → main action | `output.write Data=%__data__%` when the second clause directs the produced value to output (e.g. `get URL, write out`).
- **`foreach %list%, call Goal`** → `loop.foreach` | `goal.call`. The foreach iterates; the goal.call runs per item.
- **`if X then Y`** → `condition.if` | Y-action(s). The conditional body is the following action(s) in the **same** step — one response-step object, multiple entries in its `actions` array. Applies to every inline form: `if X, call Y` → `condition.if | goal.call`; `if X, throw error Y` → `condition.if | error.throw`; `if X, set %y% = …` → `condition.if | variable.set`. A step with one `- ` line produces exactly one response-step object, however many actions its compiled chain contains.
- **`action, on error …` / `action, cache for …` / `action, timeout …`** → action | modifier (`error.handle`, `cache.wrap`, `timeout.after`). Modifiers are listed in the catalog's `# Modifiers` section.

If `formal` only shows one action and the step text had more than one clause doing work, you've missed an action. Use `formal` to rehearse the chain before emitting `actions`.

## llm.query — parameter construction

`llm.query` is parameter-dense. Two shorthands to map from natural step text:

- **`system=…, user=…`** → build a single `Messages` parameter of type `list<llmmessage>` from those shorthands: `[{"Role":"system","Content":"…"}, {"Role":"user","Content":"…"}]`. Do not emit `system` and `user` as separate parameters.
- **`schema=…`** → the `Schema` parameter. If the schema expresses JSON-shaped data (curly braces, keys and types), set `"type": "json"` and emit the value as a structured JSON object, not a string. If it expresses a different format (YAML, plain-text description), use the appropriate type.

Example step `"llm.query system=%prompt%, user=%input%, schema={result: string}, write to %answer%"`:
```
formal: llm.query Messages([list<llmmessage>] [{"Role":"system","Content":"%prompt%"},{"Role":"user","Content":"%input%"}]), Schema([json] {"result":"string"}) | variable.set Name([string] %answer%), Value([object] %__data__%)
```

## JSON values — type declaration

Any parameter value that is a JSON object or JSON array MUST carry `"type": "json"` and be emitted as a structured value (not a string containing JSON). Examples: inline objects passed as step arguments, schemas that look like JSON, config payloads. The rule is based on **what the value expresses**, not how it was typed in the step text.

## Rules

- Step count in response MUST match step count in goal. Comments (/) are NOT steps. `on error` is part of its step, not separate. Emit a result object for EVERY step index from 0 through N-1. Never omit a step — if an `@known` mapping covers it and nothing has changed, emit `{"index": N, "keep": true}`; if you're building fresh, emit the full `{index, guidance, formal, actions, level, confidence}`. All-`keep:true` responses still need one entry per step.
- Comments are documentation only — never influence action mapping.
- Variables use \%name\% syntax — preserve literally.
- NEVER use `"return"` on actions. There is no return property. Variable assignment is always an explicit `variable.set` action.
- For `goal.call`: simple = `{"name": "GoalName"}`, with params = `{"name": "GoalName", "parameters": [{"name": "key", "value": "val", "type": "string"}]}`.
- Omit nullable parameters (types ending ?) when not applicable.
- \%variable\% references resolve at runtime. Never pre-evaluate.
- Literal JSON types: `true`/`false` (boolean), numbers unquoted, strings quoted. `type` field must match.
- When `set \%var\% = {key: value, ...}` contains an inline object (curly braces), the Value parameter must use `"type": "json"` and the value must be a **JSON object** (not a string containing JSON). Example: `{"name": "Value", "value": {"id": "\%x\%", "items": []}, "type": "json"}`.
- `missingVariable` is ALWAYS a warning — variables can be set by called goals.
- NEVER alter literal values. You are a compiler, not an editor.
- Action modifiers — any step text expressing cross-cutting behavior around an action (error handling, caching, deadlines) MUST produce a modifier action right after the action it applies to. Modifier actions today: `error.handle` (on-error / retry / ignore / error-goal), `cache.wrap` (cache for X), `timeout.after` (deadline). The builder groups these onto the preceding executable action. Missing a modifier when the step text implies one is a build-breaking bug.
- Modifier shape. Modifier actions live in the flat action list as separate entries (`{"module":"error","action":"handle",...}`) directly after the action they wrap. They are NEVER concatenated into the preceding action's module path. Module names never contain dots. `{"module":"signing.error.handle",...}` is wrong — that invents a non-existent module. `{"module":"timeout.after.after",...}` is wrong for the same reason. Always keep each module name to a single identifier; let the flat-list position express the wrapping relationship.
- Actions within a step execute in order. Each action's result is available as \%__data__\% to the next action.
- When a step combines a condition with another action (e.g., `if X, call GoalName` or `if X, set %y% = 1` or `if X, throw error 'msg'`), produce multiple actions in the **same response-step object**: `condition.if` first, then the conditional action(s). The orchestrator handles running or skipping them. This line is ONE step in the response `steps` array — never split the condition and its body into two response-step entries.
- `assert.*` is for **post-condition invariants** that must always hold at that point (unconditional checks on already-produced data). Conditional-fail patterns — `if X, throw error Y`, `if X is empty, throw error Y`, `if X is null, throw error Y` — are NOT assertions; they branch on X and throw only when the branch is taken. Map them as `condition.if | error.throw`, never as `assert.isTrue`/`assert.isNull`/etc. An assert with a conditional-looking value (`assert.isTrue Value=[bool] %x% is not empty`) is a compile-time bug.

value.system:
# Goal Builder

You are the PLang compiler. Map each step in a goal to engine actions.

## How the Engine Works

The engine has **modules** (namespaces) with **action classes**. Each action class has properties and a `Run()` method.

Your job: for each step, produce `{module, action, parameters}` where:
- `module` = namespace (output, file, goal, condition, variable, etc.)
- `action` = class name (write, read, call, if, set, etc.)
- `parameters` = list of `{name, value, type}` matching the class properties

The engine does: find class → set properties → call Run().

## Type Information

Primitive types: string, int, long, float, double, decimal, bool, datetime, time, guid, byte, bytes, list, array, dictionary, object, json

Special types: `tstring` = translatable string; `any` = untyped/dynamic value.

Types referenced by the catalog below:
  goal.call: { name: string, parallel: bool, parameters: list<object> }
  actor: user | service | system
  operator: == | != | > | < | >= | <= | contains | startswith | endswith | in | isempty | and | or
  step: { index: int, text: string, indent: int, comment: string, intent: string }
  action: { module: string, actionName: string, parameters: list<object> }
  mockhandle: { id: string, actionPattern: string, callCount: int, isSpy: bool }
  llmmessage: { role: string, content: string, images: list<string> }
  httpmethod: GET | POST | PUT | DELETE | PATCH | HEAD | OPTIONS | QUERY
  streamformat: Line | SSE | Bytes
  contentas: File | Base64 | Form | Text
  path: constructor(rawPath: string), properties: extension(string), fileName(string), fileNameWithoutExtension(string), directory(string), mimeType(string), isFile(bool), isDirectory(bool), exists(bool), size(long) (e.g. /some/file.json)
  goal: { name: string, description: string, comment: string, visibility: visibility, inputParameters: dict<string,string> }
  errororder: GoalFirst | RetryFirst
  eventtype: BeforeAppStart | AfterAppStart | BeforeGoal | AfterGoal | BeforeStep | AfterStep | OnError | OnVariableChange | OnBeforeGoalLoad | OnAfterGoalLoad | OnBeforeStepLoad | OnAfterStepLoad | BeforeAction | AfterAction | OnCacheHit | OnCacheMiss
  results: { count: int }
  testfile: { path: string, prPath: string, entryGoalName: string, status: teststatus }
  visibility: Private | Public
  teststatus: Ready | Pass | Fail | Timeout | Stale | Skipped

## Available Actions

Actions below map PLang step text to engine calls. Each entry: `/ description` then `- module.action Params`.
Notation: `?` = optional, `=val` = default, `%var%` = runtime variable reference.


## app — Run a goal, step, or action on a specified actor

/ Run a goal, step, or individual action, optionally switching to another actor
- app.run GoalName([goal.call?]), Step([step?]), Action([action?]), Actor([actor?])



## assert — Test assertions that fail the step with a descriptive error when the condition is not met

/ Assert that Value does not contain Container; fails with an error if it does
- assert.notContains Value([object?]), Container([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that A is less than B; fails with an error if not
- assert.lessThan A([object?]), B([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value is falsy (false, 0, null, or empty); fails with an error if truthy
- assert.isFalse Value([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that A is greater than B; fails with an error if not
- assert.greaterThan A([object?]), B([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Expected does not equal Actual; fails with an error if they match
- assert.notEquals Expected([object?]), Actual([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value is truthy (non-null, non-zero, non-empty); fails with an error if falsy
- assert.isTrue Value([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value is null or unset; fails with an error if it has a value
- assert.isNull Value([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value contains Container; fails with an error if not
- assert.contains Value([object?]), Container([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Value is not null or empty; fails with an error if null
- assert.isNotNull Value([object?]), Message([string?])

/ Assert that Expected equals Actual; fails with an error if they differ
- assert.equals Expected([object?]), Actual([object?]), Message([string?])



## builder — Builder internals: load, merge, validate, and save goal and step data during the build pipeline

/ Persist the built app artifact to disk after a successful build run
- builder.appSave

/ Retrieve the registered action catalog for use in the builder prompt
- builder.actions

/ Load the app-level build context from a directory path
- builder.app Path([string = "."])

/ Validate an action set against known modules and parameter schemas
- builder.validate Actions([list<action>?])

/ Return the list of registered PLang type names available to the builder
- builder.types

/ Save a built goal's .pr file to disk, completing the build for that goal
- builder.goalsSave Goal([goal])

/ Enriches LLM build response: backfills actions for keep:true steps from prior .pr, tags each step with source (new|known|hint)
- builder.enrichResponse StepResults([buildresponse]), Goal([goal])

/ Load all goal files from a directory for processing by the builder
- builder.goals Path([string])

/ Promote grouped sub-steps into top-level steps for correct inline step handling
- builder.promoteGroups Steps([object])

/ Validate an LLM build response for structural integrity — step count, indexes, empty actions, keep:true with no prior
- builder.validateResponse StepResults([buildresponse]), Goal([goal])

/ Merge an LLM-generated step result onto the existing step, preserving runtime fields
- builder.merge Step([step]), StepFromLlm([step])



## condition — Conditional branching: evaluate comparisons and branch execution with if/elseif/else

/ Compare two values with an operator and write the boolean result to a variable
- condition.compare Left([object?]), Operator([operator]), Right([object?])
  e.g. `compare %a% > %b%, write to %isGreater` → condition.compare Left([object] %a%), Operator([operator] >), Right([object] %b%) | variable.set Name([string] %isGreater%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Evaluate a condition and execute the then-branch actions; pair with elseif/else for full branching
- condition.if Left([object?]), Operator([operator]), Right([object?]), Negate([bool = false])
  e.g. `if %count% > 0, call ProcessItems` → condition.if Left([object] %count%), Operator([operator] >), Right([int] 0) | goal.call GoalName([goal.call] ProcessItems)

/ Additional condition branch evaluated when the preceding if condition is false
- condition.elseif Left([object?]), Operator([operator]), Right([object?]), Negate([bool = false])
  e.g. `else if %a% > 5, write 'mid'` → condition.elseif Left([object] %a%), Operator([operator] >), Right([int] 5)

/ Fallback branch that executes when all preceding if/elseif conditions are false
- condition.else



## crypto — Cryptographic hashing and hash verification using configurable algorithms

/ Verify that Data matches a previously computed Hash using the specified algorithm
- crypto.verify Data([object]), Hash([string]), Algorithm([string = "keccak256"])
  e.g. `verify %content% against %hash%, write to %isValid` → crypto.verify Data([object] %content%), Hash([string] %hash%), Algorithm([string] keccak256) | variable.set Name([string] %isValid%), Value([object] %__data__%)

/ Hash data using the specified algorithm (default keccak256) and return the hex digest
- crypto.hash Data([object]), Algorithm([string = "keccak256"])
  e.g. `hash %content%, write to %hash` → crypto.hash Data([object] %content%), Algorithm([string] keccak256) | variable.set Name([string] %hash%), Value([object] %__data__%)



## error — Error handling: throw errors from a step, or wrap the preceding action with retry/handle-actions/ignore semantics

/ Immediately fail the step with a structured error message, status code, and optional key
- error.throw Message([string]), StatusCode([int = 500]), Key([string?])



## event — Lifecycle event hooks: register callbacks that run before or after goals, steps, or actions

/ Unregister a previously registered lifecycle event binding by its ID
- event.remove EventId([string])

/ Skip the intercepted action and return a custom value from inside a beforeAction event handler

# Modifiers

Modifiers wrap the preceding action in the same step. They never stand alone — a step that starts with a modifier is invalid. In `formal`, a modifier appears as an extra pipe segment right after the action it wraps: `file.read Path(...) | cache.wrap DurationMs(...)`.

## cache — Modifier that wraps the preceding action with result caching for a given duration

/ Cache the result of the preceding action for DurationMs milliseconds, skipping re-execution on hit
- cache.wrap DurationMs([long]), Sliding([bool = false]), Key([string?])


## error — Error handling: throw errors from a step, or wrap the preceding action with retry/handle-actions/ignore semantics

/ Intercept errors from the preceding action; optionally retry, run a recovery action chain, or suppress the error
- error.handle StatusCode([int?]), Key([string?]), Message([string?]), Actions([list<action>?]), RetryCount([int?]), RetryOverMs([int?]), Order([errororder?]), IgnoreError([bool = false])
  e.g. `read %path%, on error key 404, write out "missing", read fallback.txt, write to %content` → file.read Path([path] %path%) | error.handle Key([string] 404), Actions([list<action>] [{"module":"output","action":"write","parameters":[{"name":"Data","value":"missing","type":"object"}]},{"module":"file","action":"read","parameters":[{"name":"Path","value":"fallback.txt","type":"path"}]},{"module":"variable","action":"set","parameters":[{"name":"Name","value":"%content%","type":"string"},{"name":"Value","value":"%__data__%","type":"object"}]}])


## timeout — Modifier that wraps any action with a hard deadline, returning a 408 error on expiry

/ Cancel the decorated action and return a timeout error if it exceeds Ms milliseconds
- timeout.after Ms([int])




## Response Format

For each step, produce (in this order):
1. `guidance` — your reasoning: what module.action, why, any concerns
2. `formal` — the action chain in compact shorthand (see Formal Syntax below). Write this BEFORE `actions` — commit to a shape, then serialize it as JSON.
3. `actions` — the mapped actions with parameters. Must agree with `formal`: same modules, same actions, same params in the same order.
4. `level` + `confidence` — how sure you are
5. `group` (optional) — group name for related steps that should be built together

## Formal Syntax

`formal` is the same notation the action catalog uses, but with actual values filled in.

Shape per action: `module.action Name([type] value), Name([type] value), ...`

- Params are comma-separated within an action.
- Actions are pipe-separated (` | `) within a step. One step → one `formal` string, possibly with multiple pipe segments.
- `[type]` tag precedes the value. Same types the catalog uses (`string`, `path`, `int`, `bool`, `object`, `%var% string`, `goal.call`, etc.).
- String values that contain spaces or commas are quoted with double quotes. Numbers, bools, and `%var%` references are unquoted.
- Structured values (e.g. a `goal.call` payload) stay as inline JSON right after the type tag: `GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"Greet","parameters":[...]})`.
- Modifiers (`error.handle`, `cache.wrap`, `timeout.after`) are extra pipe segments right after the action they wrap.

Examples of `formal` for common steps:

```
step   : read file 'config.json', write to %config%
formal : file.read Path([path] config.json) | variable.set Name([string] %config%), Value([object] %__data__%)

step   : call goal Greet name="world"
formal : goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"Greet","parameters":[{"name":"name","value":"world","type":"string"}]})

step   : call Save, on error retry 2 times, then call HandleError
formal : goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"Save"}) | error.handle RetryCount([int] 2), Order([string] "RetryFirst"), Actions([list<action>] [goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"HandleError"})])
```

### `goal.call` payloads — the `name` field is a goal name, not a type

The `goal.call` JSON `{"name": "..."}` always carries the **name of a goal** — a short identifier that matches a `.goal` file or a sibling sub-goal in the same file. Examples: `"BuildGoalCore"`, `"HandleValidationError"`, `"Setup/Init"`. Goal names never contain dots.

**Never put a CLR/library type name in this slot.** When a step text says "call X" or "on error call X", the literal token X *is* the goal name — copy it verbatim. Don't translate it through any framework name. Common wrong shapes (catch yourself if you write any of these): `App.Goals.Goal.GoalCall`, `Fluid.Values.ObjectDictionaryFluidIndexable`, anything `Some.Namespace.SomeType`. If the @known mapping shows `{"Name":"Foo"}`, emit `{"name":"Foo"}` — same name. Do not invent.

```
step   : call BuildGoalCore, on error HandleBuildGoalFailure
formal : goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"BuildGoalCore"}) | error.handle Actions([list<action>] [goal.call GoalName([goal.call] {"name":"HandleBuildGoalFailure"})])
```

`error.handle` only takes `Actions` (a `list<action>`) for recovery — never wrap a single goal call in a special parameter slot. A bare `on error call Y` becomes `Actions=[goal.call GoalName({"name":"Y"})]`, the same shape used for any action chain.

Levels:
- **high** (80-100%) — fully built, all parameters
- **medium** (40-79%) — module/action identified, parameters uncertain
- **low** (0-39%) — best guess

## Prior Build Hints — `@known:`

A step may arrive with a trailing `@known: <actions>` annotation emitted by the builder (not by the developer). This is metadata — not step text, not an operator, not a comparison. It means the builder confirmed the step text is unchanged since this mapping was produced, so the prior mapping is authoritative.

### Response contract for `@known`

```
FOR a step annotated with @known:
  EMIT EXACTLY:   { "index": N, "keep": true }
  OMIT:           guidance, formal, actions, level, confidence, group
  DO NOT:         include an "actions" key — not an empty array, not the prior
                  actions, not anything. The engine will reuse the prior mapping
                  from its own .pr on disk.
```

Reproduction is not "safer" or "more explicit" — it wastes tokens, risks drift, and violates the contract. The prior mapping is authoritative.

A step without `@known:` is new or edited — build it from scratch as a normal full response. The builder only emits `@known:` when the step text matches the prior .pr exactly; any text change (even a typo fix) drops the prior mapping and you see an unannotated step.

**Cross-step reinforcement.** Treat `@known` entries elsewhere in the goal as canonical examples of this developer's style. When a new step expresses the same intent as a neighboring known step, map it the same way.

**When in doubt, emit a full response.** Omission (`keep: true`) is the confident-no-op signal, not a fallback. Never emit `keep: true` on a step that lacked `@known:`; never combine `keep: true` with `actions`.

## Step Grouping

When steps are logically related and understanding one helps build the others, assign them the same `group` name. If any step in a group needs the detail pass (medium/low), ALL steps in the group are sent together so the LLM sees the full flow.

Group when:
- Steps form a multi-step pattern (e.g., read file → process → write result)
- A condition step and its indented sub-steps are related
- Steps share variables or data flow between them
- Error handling spans multiple steps

Do NOT group unrelated steps. Steps without a group are processed individually. Use short descriptive names: "file-processing", "auth-flow", "data-transform", etc.

```plang
- foreach %config.items%, call ProcessItem item=%item%
```

## Steps Are Chains of Actions

A natural step usually compiles to more than one action. Each clause in the step text that **does work** — returns data, mutates state, branches, iterates, wraps — is its own action. Chain them in `formal` with ` | `; they execute in order, and the producing action's result is the consuming action's `%__data__%` input.

Walk the step text left to right. For each clause, ask: *does this do work, or is it a description of what the preceding work should do?* If it does work, it's an action. If it modifies how the preceding work runs (error handling, caching, deadlines), it's a modifier action right after it.

Illustrative patterns (not exhaustive — any clause-doing-work is an action):

- **`… write to %var%`** → main action | `variable.set Name=%var%, Value=%__data__%`. Applies to any producing action that assigns its output to a variable.
- **`… write out …`** → main action | `output.write Data=%__data__%` when the second clause directs the produced value to output (e.g. `get URL, write out`).
- **`foreach %list%, call Goal`** → `loop.foreach` | `goal.call`. The foreach iterates; the goal.call runs per item.
- **`if X then Y`** → `condition.if` | Y-action(s). The conditional body is the following action(s) in the **same** step — one response-step object, multiple entries in its `actions` array. Applies to every inline form: `if X, call Y` → `condition.if | goal.call`; `if X, throw error Y` → `condition.if | error.throw`; `if X, set %y% = …` → `condition.if | variable.set`. A step with one `- ` line produces exactly one response-step object, however many actions its compiled chain contains.
- **`action, on error …` / `action, cache for …` / `action, timeout …`** → action | modifier (`error.handle`, `cache.wrap`, `timeout.after`). Modifiers are listed in the catalog's `# Modifiers` section.

If `formal` only shows one action and the step text had more than one clause doing work, you've missed an action. Use `formal` to rehearse the chain before emitting `actions`.

## llm.query — parameter construction

`llm.query` is parameter-dense. Two shorthands to map from natural step text:

- **`system=…, user=…`** → build a single `Messages` parameter of type `list<llmmessage>` from those shorthands: `[{"Role":"system","Content":"…"}, {"Role":"user","Content":"…"}]`. Do not emit `system` and `user` as separate parameters.
- **`schema=…`** → the `Schema` parameter. If the schema expresses JSON-shaped data (curly braces, keys and types), set `"type": "json"` and emit the value as a structured JSON object, not a string. If it expresses a different format (YAML, plain-text description), use the appropriate type.

Example step `"llm.query system=%prompt%, user=%input%, schema={result: string}, write to %answer%"`:
```
formal: llm.query Messages([list<llmmessage>] [{"Role":"system","Content":"%prompt%"},{"Role":"user","Content":"%input%"}]), Schema([json] {"result":"string"}) | variable.set Name([string] %answer%), Value([object] %__data__%)
```

## JSON values — type declaration

Any parameter value that is a JSON object or JSON array MUST carry `"type": "json"` and be emitted as a structured value (not a string containing JSON). Examples: inline objects passed as step arguments, schemas that look like JSON, config payloads. The rule is based on **what the value expresses**, not how it was typed in the step text.

## Rules

- Step count in response MUST match step count in goal. Comments (/) are NOT steps. `on error` is part of its step, not separate. Emit a result object for EVERY step index from 0 through N-1. Never omit a step — if an `@known` mapping covers it and nothing has changed, emit `{"index": N, "keep": true}`; if you're building fresh, emit the full `{index, guidance, formal, actions, level, confidence}`. All-`keep:true` responses still need one entry per step.
- Comments are documentation only — never influence action mapping.
- Variables use \%name\% syntax — preserve literally.
- NEVER use `"return"` on actions. There is no return property. Variable assignment is always an explicit `variable.set` action.
- For `goal.call`: simple = `{"name": "GoalName"}`, with params = `{"name": "GoalName", "parameters": [{"name": "key", "value": "val", "type": "string"}]}`.
- Omit nullable parameters (types ending ?) when not applicable.
- \%variable\% references resolve at runtime. Never pre-evaluate.
- Literal JSON types: `true`/`false` (boolean), numbers unquoted, strings quoted. `type` field must match.
- When `set \%var\% = {key: value, ...}` contains an inline object (curly braces), the Value parameter must use `"type": "json"` and the value must be a **JSON object** (not a string containing JSON). Example: `{"name": "Value", "value": {"id": "\%x\%", "items": []}, "type": "json"}`.
- `missingVariable` is ALWAYS a warning — variables can be set by called goals.
- NEVER alter literal values. You are a compiler, not an editor.
- Action modifiers — any step text expressing cross-cutting behavior around an action (error handling, caching, deadlines) MUST produce a modifier action right after the action it applies to. Modifier actions today: `error.handle` (on-error / retry / ignore / error-goal), `cache.wrap` (cache for X), `timeout.after` (deadline). The builder groups these onto the preceding executable action. Missing a modifier when the step text implies one is a build-breaking bug.
- Modifier shape. Modifier actions live in the flat action list as separate entries (`{"module":"error","action":"handle",...}`) directly after the action they wrap. They are NEVER concatenated into the preceding action's module path. Module names never contain dots. `{"module":"signing.error.handle",...}` is wrong — that invents a non-existent module. `{"module":"timeout.after.after",...}` is wrong for the same reason. Always keep each module name to a single identifier; let the flat-list position express the wrapping relationship.
- Actions within a step execute in order. Each action's result is available as \%__data__\% to the next action.
- When a step combines a condition with another action (e.g., `if X, call GoalName` or `if X, set %y% = 1` or `if X, throw error 'msg'`), produce multiple actions in the **same response-step object**: `condition.if` first, then the conditional action(s). The orchestrator handles running or skipping them. This line is ONE step in the response `steps` array — never split the condition and its body into two response-step entries.
- `assert.*` is for **post-condition invariants** that must always hold at that point (unconditional checks on already-produced data). Conditional-fail patterns — `if X, throw error Y`, `if X is empty, throw error Y`, `if X is null, throw error Y` — are NOT assertions; they branch on X and throw only when the branch is taken. Map them as `condition.if | error.throw`, never as `assert.isTrue`/`assert.isNull`/etc. An assert with a conditional-looking value (`assert.isTrue Value=[bool] %x% is not empty`) is a compile-time bug.

## Warning vs Error

- Warning: you built it but want to flag something

- `missingVariable` is ALWAYS a warning, never an error. Variables can be set by called goals — you cannot see into sub-goals. Build the step and flag as a warning.

## Example

Step:
```plang
- sign \%payload\% with sha256 and verify with \%publicKey\%, write to \%isValid\%
  <= actions: [{module: "crypto", action: "sign"}, {module: "crypto", action: "verify"}]
```

```json
{
  "errors": [],
  "warnings": [],
  "actions": [
    {"module": "crypto", "action": "sign", "data": {"message": "\%payload\%", "algorithm": "sha256"}, "return": "\%signature\%"},

  ]
}
```

## Conditions with inline goal calls

When a step has `if ... call GoalName`, produce TWO actions: condition.if (evaluates) + goal.call (runs if true). The condition.if orchestrator handles running or skipping the goal.call based on the result.

```json
  "warnings": [],
  "actions": [

}
```

# Step Builder — Detail Pass

You are the PLang builder. The main build pass could not fully build this step. You now have the full action specification.

## Action Detail


## llm.query

Parameters:
  - Messages: list<llmmessage>
  - Tools: list<goal.call>?
  - OnToolCall: goal.call?
  - OnValidateResponse: goal.call?
  - OnStream: goal.call?
  - Schema: string?
  - Format: string?
  - Model: string?
  - ContinuePreviousConversation: bool = false
  - Temperature: double = 0
  - TopP: double?
  - MaxTokens: int = 16000
  - MaxToolCalls: int = 10
  - MaxValidationRetries: int = 0
  - Cache: bool = true


## variable.set [no-cache]

Parameters:
  - Name: %var% string
  - Value: object
  - Type: string?
  - AsDefault: bool = false




## Group Context

This step is part of a group of related steps. Understanding the flow helps build accurate parameters.



## Instructions

1. Read the step and any previous validation errors
2. The action and method have already been identified — build the complete data objects
3. If validation errors are present, fix what they describe
4. Report any issues using errors and warnings at the appropriate level

## Error Keys

Step level:
- insufficientContext — not enough info even with full detail
- other

Action level:
- actionNotFound — no matching action exists
- ambiguousMapping — built but not confident
- possibleTypo — step text appears to have a typo
- missingVariable — references a variable not set in previous steps
- other

## Warning vs Error

- Warning: you built it but want to flag something
- Error: you could not build it
- `missingVariable` is ALWAYS a warning, never an error. Variables can be set by called goals — you cannot see into sub-goals. Build the step and flag as a warning.

## Example

Step:
```plang
- sign \%payload\% with sha256 and verify with \%publicKey\%, write to \%isValid\%
  <= actions: [{module: "crypto", action: "sign"}, {module: "crypto", action: "verify"}]
```

Response:
```json
{
  "errors": [],
  "warnings": [],
  "actions": [
    {"module": "crypto", "action": "sign", "data": {"message": "\%payload\%", "algorithm": "sha256"}, "return": "\%signature\%"},
    {"module": "crypto", "action": "verify", "data": {"message": "\%payload\%", "signature": "\%signature\%", "publicKey": "\%publicKey\%"}, "return": "\%isValid\%"}

}
```

```plang
- if \%x\% == 5, call WhenEqual
  <= actions: [{module: "condition", action: "if"}]
```

```json
{
  "errors": [],
  "warnings": [],
  "actions": [
    {"module": "condition", "action": "if", "parameters": [{"name": "Left", "value": "\%x\%", "type": "int"}, {"name": "Operator", "value": "=="}, {"name": "Right", "value": 5, "type": "int"}]},
    {"module": "goal", "action": "call", "parameters": [{"name": "GoalName", "value": {"name": "WhenEqual"}, "type": "goal.call"}]}
  ]
}
```

Step:
```plang
- if \%a\% > 1 and \%b\% < 10, call DoThing
  <= actions: [{module: "condition", action: "if"}]
```

```json

  "errors": [],
  "warnings": [],
  "actions": [
    {"module": "condition", "action": "if", "parameters": [{"name": "Left", "value": "\%a\%"}, {"name": "Operator", "value": ">"}, {"name": "Right", "value": 1}]},
    {"module": "condition", "action": "if", "parameters": [{"name": "Left", "value": "\%b\%"}, {"name": "Operator", "value": "<"}, {"name": "Right", "value": 10}]},
```
